[American Express / Senior Associate Product Manager] As Senior Associate Product Manager at American Express (Jul-Sep 2024), Satvik led the end-to-end CDD platform vendor evaluation. Assessed NICE Actimize, Symphony AI (BAE Solutions CRR product), and SAS against functional fit, configurability, integration capability, cost, and scalability. Down-selected from 15+ to 8 to 3 to 2 finalists. Ran POC alongside in-house build. Presented recommendation to VP Gregory Liss and secured sign-off for in-house build. Received Leadership in Action Award from SVP Nerses Kazarian.
[American Express / Senior Associate Product Manager] At American Express (Nov-Dec 2024), Satvik decomposed 21 BRD requirements (12.1-12.21 covering CRR Framework, Configurability, Audit Trail, Sandbox, AI/ML Guidelines, etc.) into 6 core capabilities: Scoring Engine Configuration (drag-and-drop rule builder), Asset Manager, Sandbox Environment, Reporting & Dashboards, Audit Trail, and Customer Scoring View. Organized into 3 parallel workstreams. Built comprehensive 3-year roadmap from PI 25.1 through 25.5.
[American Express / Senior Associate Product Manager] At American Express (2024-2025), Satvik led an 18-member scrum team (12 Rule Configuration + 6 Rule Execution) across 10 sprints without any manager or direct supervision. Delivered 4 consecutive zero-spillover PIs, 60+ features. Reduced speed-to-market from 10 days to 3 days (~70% reduction). Operated under SAFe framework with quarterly PI Planning cycles.
[American Express / Senior Associate Product Manager] At American Express (2024-2025), Satvik drove 20+ UX research sessions with compliance analysts and managers across 6 regions. Designed 3 new capability UIs end-to-end: Asset Manager (centralized reference list management), Advanced Rule Configurations (drag-and-drop rule builder), and Sandbox Versioning (simulation and testing environment). Used Figma for all designs.
[American Express / Senior Associate Product Manager] At American Express (PI 26.1-present), Satvik designed the Consolidated Data Layer strategy after CRR was blocked by unstandardized data. Scoped 54 data points across Individual and Entity/Intermediary products. For Product Type: 136,738 raw records → 12,408 standardized → 64 categories (2,137:1 compression). For Industry Type: 521,583 raw → 42,182 standardized → 98 categories. Built 5-step pipeline: Translation, Standardization, Categorization, Derivation, Master List. Roadmap spans PI 26.2-27.5. Team of 4 with direct leadership visibility.
[American Express / Senior Associate Product Manager] At American Express (2025), Satvik navigated 2 major re-baselining events. First: leadership compressed 2-year modernization to 1 year — bare minimum to decommission Cadence. Satvik reprioritized entire backlog in 1 week. Second: CRR build stopped when supporting data team underperformed; pivoted to CDL data strategy. Satvik had already started pre-work on data standardization, giving the team a head start.
[American Express / Senior Associate Product Manager] At American Express (2024-2025), Satvik onboarded and ramped 4 resources: 3 Product Owners (Tahcin, Suprana, Sakshi) and 1 UX designer (Shivani, hired through 12-round competitive selection process, reports directly to Satvik).
[American Express / Senior Associate Product Manager] At American Express (January 2025), Satvik captained an 8-member team at the 2-day Growth Hack innovation event. Built an AI Backlog Grooming bot — a Slack-integrated tool using AI combined with Rally to auto-write user stories and product features. Ranked #21 out of 400+ teams (Top 6%). Concept was later productionized by GMNS as QE Assist.
[Sprinklr / Senior Product Implementation Consultant] At Sprinklr (2023-2024) as Senior Implementation Consultant, Satvik built the Sharek iOS app for Qatar's PM office as part of a $32M, 5-year CXM engagement across 40+ ministries. App enabled PM's office to monitor citizen complaints using NLP sentiment analysis. Delivered insights within 4 weeks of project initiation. Led 5-member RTL localization team. 7-day retention improved 40%→55%. Time-to-insight reduced 2 days→2 hours. Improved Sentiment model F1 by 11% and Intuition model by 13% for Arabic. Qatar advanced from rank 78→53 on UN E-Government Index.
[Sprinklr / Senior Product Implementation Consultant] At Sprinklr (2023) during the Qatar CGB project, Satvik scaled team from 6 to 14 consultants in 3 months using a structured 'reverse-KT' (reverse Knowledge Transfer) onboarding process where new joiners actively extracted knowledge through a systematic framework and taught it back. Led team of 8 to deliver 120 personalized dashboards for 40 government ministries in 4 weeks. Managed 21 display screens for real-time monitoring.
[Sprinklr / Senior Product Implementation Consultant] At Sprinklr (2024) as Senior Implementation Consultant, Satvik built a GenAI root-cause product for Walmart Spark Driver Support. Analyzed 100K+ contact center calls using unsupervised ML clustering and correlation detection. Designed custom L1/L2/L3 issue taxonomy. Built unified data architecture joining internal support data with public social data via SQL. Ran live Black Friday pilot with dedicated WhatsApp group for Walmart stakeholders. Time-to-insight: 7 days→same-day. Revenue: $1.2M TCV from POC-to-deal, $9M+ pipeline for Q4 FY24. Walmart later signed $2M follow-on deal for Last Mile Delivery.
[Sprinklr / Senior Product Analyst] At Sprinklr (2024) as Senior Product Analyst, Satvik grew Use Case Hub adoption from 35% to 85% across 1,500+ SME SaaS clients (<$1M spend). Prioritized 10 use cases across 3 MVPs using product adoption data. Built 'Listen, Learn, and Act' framework as core architecture. Eliminated 7-day manual configuration with self-serve setup (under 10 seconds). 75% TTV reduction. 15% revenue increase in first 3 months. Covered 15 industry verticals with ~10 sub-verticals each.
[Sprinklr / Senior Product Implementation Consultant] At Sprinklr (2024), the team wanted an AI model to auto-categorize social listening data into themes. The model had bias — strong on viral trends but missed emerging/nuanced stories. Director told Satvik to wait for the model. Over a single weekend, Satvik built a system of prompts using prompt engineering to go from broad categories to sub-categories to optimized keyword Boolean queries. Validated against pre-labeled data with defined success metrics. Produced 60,000 themes. Director approved as 'temporary' solution — it remains in production years later with no model replacement.
[Sprinklr / Product Implementation Consultant] At Sprinklr (April 2022 - March 2023) as Product Implementation Consultant, Satvik secured Rank 1 and Gold Medal globally among all 28 Implementation Consultants worldwide. Received fast-tracked promotion to Senior Implementation Consultant within 11 months. Delivered key results: Adobe TTV reduced by 75%, custom AI spam detection model with F1=87% for Adobe, 15% month-over-month brand engagement increase for Snapchat, 25% brand reputation risk reduction for Discord.
[Personal / Mentor] After Sprinklr restructuring, Satvik hosted a laid-off junior colleague (from farming family in Rajasthan, JNV + Super 30, NIT Warangal graduate) for 3 months. Conducted 15-20 mock interviews, coached on reframing implementation experience as product work, connected him with PM network. Colleague secured Associate Product Manager role at NxtWave without a single day of unemployment.
[Personal / Mentor] Satvik guided his sister Sanika's career from JEE preparation through professional placement. Ensured she chose coding-focused branch (ECE at IIT BHU) after learning from his own mistake of choosing Civil Engineering. Sanika became the only girl to secure Google internship from IIT BHU, topped girls' category in Electronics (9.2 GPA). Pushed her to apply to Confluent (reached 6th round). Now Senior Software Developer at Oracle.
[IIT Delhi / Student] In JEE Advanced 2017, Satvik forgot to fill paper code mid-exam, experienced 3-4 minute psychological breakdown (blurred vision, ringing ears). Recovered by asking 'what's the worst that could happen?' Scored 111 in Paper 1 and 127 in Paper 2. Lost ~16 marks to disorientation in Paper 2 settling phase. Final rank: 2446 out of 12 lakh candidates (top 0.002%). JEE Mains rank: 9713. Dramatic improvement Mains→Advanced reflected deep conceptual problem-solving strength.
[Sprinklr / Senior Product Analyst] At Sprinklr (May-July 2024) as Senior Product Analyst, Satvik developed the product strategy and FY-25 roadmap for Advocacy — an employee advocacy platform with 182 customers, growing from $7M ARR in Q2 2024 to $9.8M ARR in Q3 2024 (40% YoY growth). Covered 5 business pillars, competitive landscape against FirstUp, EveryoneSocial, Sprout Social, DSMN8, Haiilo in a $400-700M market. Built quarterly feature roadmap across Advocacy, Integrations, Social Recruiting, and Employee Communication.
[Sukha Education Foundation / Strategy Consultant (GGI)] As strategy consultant through Global Governance Initiative (Jan-Apr 2025), Satvik delivered digital transformation strategy for Sukha Education Foundation, an NGO teaching underprivileged children English in Chennai. Addressed process fragmentation across 50+ volunteers. Recommended Google Workspace, Slack, and Salesforce CRM implementation. Result: ~Rs 60,000 in annual cost savings through automation and process optimization, implemented in 12-week phased rollout.
[LinkRight / Founder / Builder] Since June 2025, Satvik has been building LinkRight — an agentic AI career navigation suite. Products include: Sync (resume signal engineering and JD optimization), Flex (LinkedIn content pipeline), Scout (job portal monitoring), Shipquick (professional document builder — demoed to VPs at Amex), and AutoFlow (n8n workflow automation). Vision: automate entire lifecycle of landing a top-tier tech job from raw experience to structured career signals to tailored application artifacts.
[American Express / Senior Associate Product Manager] At American Express (2025), Satvik drove ChatGPT Enterprise adoption within his team, achieving 80% AI-assisted story drafting for sprint planning and backlog management. Also built Shipquick (agentic AI document builder) and demoed it to VPs, which led to a stretch role on the Negative News and Adverse Media Triage Agent project integrating Work Fusion's EvantAgent.
[Sprinklr / Product Implementation Consultant] At Sprinklr (2022-2023) as Product Implementation Consultant, Satvik reduced Time To Value by 75% for Adobe by unifying unstructured social media data and automating data analysis using SQL and Data Pipelines. Managed entire data pipeline lifecycle: extraction, cleaning, transformation, and unification of Earned, Organic, and Paid data using SQL and Python. Built and deployed a custom AI spam detection model achieving F1 Score of 87%.
[Sprinklr / Product Implementation Consultant] At Sprinklr (2022-2023) as Product Implementation Consultant, Satvik reduced brand reputation risk by 25% for Discord by building a custom AI-based detection system for identifying hate comments on Discord servers.
[Sprinklr / Senior Product Implementation Consultant] At Sprinklr (2023-2024) as Senior Implementation Consultant, Satvik improved customer happiness to 9.14/10 across 36 projects in 15+ industry verticals. Reduced churn rate from 13% to 9% by achieving perfect 10/10 CHI score on 66% of projects. Generated $1.6M in direct consultant timecard revenue.
[Sprinklr / Senior Product Implementation Consultant] At Sprinklr (2023-2024) as Senior Implementation Consultant, Satvik improved product usability of Sprinklr's CXM platform on Rolex's $2M account, leading development of 11+ product enhancement requests. This was product-level work above IC responsibilities, collaborating with product and data science teams to create customized ML models.
[ContentStack / AI Product Manager] At ContentStack (Nov 2024-Jun 2025) as AI Product Manager (concurrent with Amex role), Satvik helped develop and design 3 AI product tools: Compose AI (AI content creation), DesignerAI (AI website creation), and Lens (website performance monitoring).
[GoGoGo / Investor & Advisor] From September 2024 to June 2025 (concurrent with Amex), Satvik served as investor and advisor at GoGoGo, a ride-hailing startup competing with Ola/Uber. Declined co-founder role due to bandwidth. Helped build team, met co-founders, investors, and advisors. Gained exposure to fundraising, team building, regulatory compliance. GoGoGo operational in Indore, expanding to 11 cities, raised ~Rs 2.5 crore pre-seed.
[IIT Delhi / Graduate] In GATE 2022 (Civil Engineering), Satvik scored 65/100 (68 raw, 65 normalized), securing AIR ~1463, top 1.5% of 100K+ candidates. Missed PSU cutoff by 6-7 marks. Had declined PwC offer (6-7 LPA) to prepare for GATE, betting on PSU placement (15-16 LPA). This was a calculated risk after graduating from IIT Delhi with 6.43 CGPA.
[S.S.M Sr. Secondary School / Student] In MPBSE Class 12 (2017), Satvik secured Rank 1 in entire Chhatarpur District with 94.2%. Scored perfect 100/100 in both Mathematics and Chemistry, earning Rs 5,000 merit scholarship. Achieved this with only 1 month of board exam preparation while primarily focused on JEE coaching in Kota. Near-miss for top 10 in state (4 marks short).
[Global Tech Initiative / Fellow] Satvik completed the Feynman Fellowship at Global Tech Initiative, a pre-MBA program led by former BCG consultant Naman Srivastava. The program covered management consulting, product management, corporate policy, and startups. Satvik won the startup weekend competition and was a top performer in the cohort.
[Personal / Content Creator] At American Express (2025), Satvik gained 8,000-9,000 LinkedIn followers within 2-3 months through authentic personal branding — sharing daily PM experiences, growth and learnings, mentoring, job search advice, and building a leadership voice.
[Personal / Family] On July 8, 2010, Satvik's father (a Canara Bank manager) took his own life when Satvik was 11. Satvik was thrust into the role of eldest and only male in the family, responsible for mother and younger sister Sanika. This tragedy shaped his resilience, determination, and deep understanding of mental health. He essentially grew up ten years overnight.
[IIT Delhi / Student] Satvik completed 5 years of Indian Classical Music vocal training culminating in a Junior Diploma, started by his father in childhood and continued through IIT Delhi. He plays the Harmonium. Secured Rank 2 in group song competition at IIT Delhi (Udaigiri hostel vs 12 hostels). Also won Rank 2 in wall painting competition at IIT Delhi and Rank 1 three times in community Navratri drawing competitions.
